title: Java IO Streams
description: Read text line by line through a BufferedReader with try-with-resources.
learningGoals: Wrap a character stream in a BufferedReader and read it one line at a time.
Release the underlying stream automatically with try-with-resources.

completionChecks.prompt: What does reader.readLine() return to end the loop?
completionChecks.explanation: BufferedReader.readLine returns null at end of stream; an empty line would be an empty string.

completionChecks.prompt: Why is BufferedReader in a try-with-resources statement?
choices: It makes readLine skip blank lines
completionChecks.explanation: try-with-resources closes the reader when the block exits, including when an exception is thrown.

completionChecks.prompt: What prints while the BufferedReader loop runs?
completionChecks.explanation: Each non-null line is uppercased and printed separately; the null end marker is used only to stop the loop.
